title_en: Bill 96 — An Act respecting French, the official and common language of Québec
title_fr: Loi sur la langue officielle et commune du Québec, le français (Loi 96)
administered_by: Office québécois de la langue française (OQLF)
description_en: Bill 96 significantly strengthens the Charter of the French Language in Quebec. It imposes new obligations on businesses regarding the language of work, commerce, and service delivery.

key_obligations_for_businesses.description_en: Employees have the right to work in French. Employers with 25+ employees must have a Francisation (Frenchification) program approved by the OQLF.
Public signage must be in French. Other languages may appear if French is markedly predominant (at least twice the size). Trade names must have a French version.
Contracts with Quebec consumers must be offered in French first. Job postings, offer letters, and internal communications must be in French.
key_obligations_for_businesses.consumer_contracts: French version must be offered before signing

key_obligations_for_businesses.description_en: Businesses must offer French-language software to employees where a French equivalent exists. Websites serving Quebec customers must be available in French.
